We need your help! We have a Human Library Survey that we’d appreciate if people could fill out. You can find more info and fill out the Human Library Survey here.
There’s always so much going on in the Teen Dept. from Anime Club to Book Babble, and more! Don’t forget to check out our Teens Groups & Clubs page to see all the good stuff that happens every month.
Looking for Children’s Programs? Check out our Youth Dept. Program Calendar.
Looking for something for your kiddos to read? Check out our Suggested Reading Lists
And watch our Calendar of Events. Sign ups for the June Summer Reading programs start on May 15!
Support the Friends of the Nevins Library by visiting the Chateau Restaurants in Andover for lunch, dinner, or take-out on Tuesday, June 16th. The Friends will receive a percentage of the total sales (before tax) from all the checks that were flagged for the fundraiser. Find more information about the Chateau Fundraiser here.

pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "105 Haverhill Road, Salem, NH 03079","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/nh-americas-stonehenge_288_md.png","telephone": "603-893-8300","name": "America&apos;s Stonehenge","url": "","description": "&ldquo;America&apos;s Stonehenge,&rdquo; identified as an accurate astronomical calendar, was built over 4,000 years ago. Its man-made chambers, walls, and ceremonial meeting places were constructed by people well-versed in astronomy and stone construction. Visitors explore the site as a self-guided tour. (A phone app can be downloaded before arrival that contains an audio tour and more detailed information that the paper map which may acquired on site.) Note that, because of the uneven and rocky terrain of the site, only the visitor center and gift shop are handicap accessible."} America's Stonehenge 105 Haverhill Road, Salem, NH 03079603-893-8300https://www.stonehengeusa.com/[Details/Reserve]
America's Stonehenge “America's Stonehenge,” identified as an accurate astronomical calendar, was built over 4,000 years ago. Its man-made chambers, walls, and ceremonial meeting places were constructed by people well-versed in astronomy and stone construction. Visitors explore the site as a self-guided tour. (A phone app can be downloaded before arrival that contains an audio tour and more detailed information that the paper map which may acquired on site.) Note that, because of the uneven and rocky terrain of the site, only the visitor center and gift shop are handicap accessible.
Categories: Family, History, Science, Technology
Pass Type: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
Pass Benefits This pass entitles the bearer to half-price admission for up to four people to America's Stonehenge on the date noted on the pass. In addition, the pass entitles the bearer to a 10% discount on gift shop purchases, discounts on special programs and events and discounts on snowshoe rentals. 
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "27 Navigator Road, Londonderry, NH 03053","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/nh-the-aviation-museum-of-new-hampshire_271_md.png","telephone": "603-669-4820","name": "The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire","url": "","description": "The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire is housed in an art deco terminal and control tower building in Londonderry. The historic building was constructed as a WPA project in 1937 and was moved in 2004 to its present location on the southeast side of the Manchester Airport before being renovated. Hands-on exhibits tell the story of New Hampshire aviation history and include a flight simulator, various historical artifacts, and planes and models."} The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire 27 Navigator Road, Londonderry, NH 03053603-669-4820https://aviationmuseumofnh.org/[Details/Reserve]
The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire The Aviation Museum of New Hampshire is housed in an art deco terminal and control tower building in Londonderry. The historic building was constructed as a WPA project in 1937 and was moved in 2004 to its present location on the southeast side of the Manchester Airport before being renovated. Hands-on exhibits tell the story of New Hampshire aviation history and include a flight simulator, various historical artifacts, and planes and models.
Categories: Family, History, Technology, Sky
Pass Benefits Coupon pass admits four visitors to the Aviation Museum of New Hampshire free of charge.
Regular admission rates are $15 for people 13 and older, $7.50 for seniors, veterans, and children 6-12. Children 5 years old and younger are always free.
Pass is only valid on the day of the reservation.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-childrens-museum_3_md.jpg","telephone": "617-426-6500","name": "Boston Children&apos;s Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers&apos; Bureau, the Boston Children&apos;s Museum is the second oldest children&apos;s museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Admits up to 4 (adults or children) at half price per person.
After receiving a library discount pass, you must reserve a visit online with Boston Children’s Museum. Due to safety measures, walk-ins are not allowed. Museum capacity is limited at this time.
    Patrons must first acquire a dated library discount pass.
    Then visit https://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/ticketing
    Review the directions for timed tickets
    Click on the "Discount Programs" & drop down arrow
    Click on "Half Price Library Timed Ticket " to book
    NEW - Online reservations are required to visit PlaySpace.
    The date on the library discount pass must match the date of your visit.
    After arriving at the Museum, proceed to the admissions scanning station with your online reservation and the date specific library discount pass. If you have questions about the online ordering system, contact Orders@BostonChildrensMuseum.org.
This e-ticket is valid only for one day. You can either print it out and take it with you or display the pass on your phone or device. Either way, please be sure that the pass barcode is visible.

Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there."} Boston Harbor Island Ferry 15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109617-223-8666http://www.bostonharborislands.org/[Details/Reserve]
